title: eremophila forrestii subsp. hastieana
intro: Eremophila forrestii subsp. hastieana is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae, commonly known as a type of emu bush. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian folk medicine to treat various health issues due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Eremophila forrestii subsp. hastieana include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable herb for wound healing and immune system support. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tincture, infusion, or dried herb to harness its medicinal properties.
